content.full_text:
May 19, 1975


Mr. George H. Murphy
Legislative Counsel
3021 State Capitol
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Murphy:

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 75-114

Thank you for your letter of May 5, 1975 requesting an opinion under the Political Reform Act. You asked whether the Act prohibits a member of the Legislature from accepting an invitation to dinner and entertainment from an association which employs a lobbyist. The cost of the program will exceed ten dollars. If the legislator can accept the invitation, you asked whether he or she is required to disclose his acceptance.

Your request poses questions involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act which will be answered in opinions pending before the Commission. Therefore, your request is denied.

Your request does not describe what role, if any, the lobbyist employed by the Regional Council of Rural Counties has played in planning the dinner. Government Code Section 86203 prohibits a lobbyist from making gifts to a legislator aggregating more than ten dollars in a calendar month, from acting as an agent or intermediary in the making of any gift, or from arranging the making of any gift.

If a member of the Legislature does attend the dinner, he or she need not disclose the acceptance unless the value of the dinner and other gifts received from the Regional Council aggregates twenty-five dollars or more over the period covered by the disclosure statement. If so, the legislator must report the value of the gifts and dates of receipt, and disclose the name, address and

Mr. George H. Murphy
May 19, 1975
Page Two


general description of the business activity of the source
on the Statement of Economic Interests (Form 720) (see
Section 87207).

Although no formal opinion will be issued, I hope the
foregoing will be helpful, and direct your attention to
the opinion request of Thomas F. Olson, representative, the
California Farm Bureau Federation (No. 75-057), presently
pending before the Commission. If you have any further
questions, I urge you to contact a member of the Commission
staff.

Sincerely,


Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

BERNARD CZESLA
CHIEF DEPUTY

OWEN K. HARRIS
EDWARD H. PURCELL
RAY H. WHITACRE

T. L. DCHAMBEAU
1ST M. KNOLL
ALEY M. LOURIMORE
MERWIN C. FLETCHER, JR.
JACK KNOX
EDWARD F. NOWAK
RUSSELL L. SPAULDING
PRINCIPAL DEPUTIES

3021 STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO 95814

107 SOUTH BROADWAY
LOS ANGELES 90012

Received 5/23/75                    GERALD ROSS ADAMS
                                   DAVID D. ALVES
                                   MARTIN L. ANDERSON
                                   WILLIAM AYRES
                                   JEFFREY D. ARTHUR
                                   CHARLES G. AMES
                                   JAMES L. ASHFORD
                                   JOHN COSSING
                                   JOHN CONLAN
                                   CLINTON J. DEWITT
                                   D. DAVIDエRICKSON
                                   FRANCES E. DOOTSON
                                   ROBERT CULLEN DUFFY
                                   EVERETT DIRKSEN
                                   LAWRENCE H. FEIN
                                   JOHN FIORETTI
                                   HARVEY J. FOSTER
                                   HENRY CLAY FULLER III
                                   ROBERT J. GABLE
                                   ROBERT D. GRONNE
                                   JAMES W. HEINZER
                                   THOMAS R. HEUER
                                   MICHAEL J. KEHOLAN
                                   JOHN KIMBLE
                                   JEAN KLINGENSMITH
                                   VICTOR KEJELSKI
                                   STEPHEN E. LENZI
                                   DANIEL LOUIS
                                   JOE LOPEZ
                                   PETER F. McINICK
                                   MIRKO A. MILOSEVICH
                                   VERNEL OLIVER
                                   EUGENE L. PAINE
                                   RICHARDSON PATTEE
                                   MARGUERITE ROTH
                                   HUGH P. QUACKENBUSH
                                   MARY SHAW
                                   JOHN T. STIEDEBECK
                                   WILLIAM THORNTON
                                   BRIAN L. WALKUP
                                   THOMAS D. WHELAN
                                   JIMMIE WING
                                   CHRISTOPHER ZIRKLE
                                   DEPUTIES

Legislative Counsel
of California

GEORGE H. MURPHY


Sacramento, California
May 5, 1975


Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street, Room 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Request No. 75-2

Dear Commissioners:

     Pursuant to Section 83114 of the Government Code,
I request your opinion on the following questions based on
the given facts:

                              FACTS

     The Regional Council of Rural Counties, an associa-
tion of county supervisors, plans to invite members of the
legislature to a program of dinner and entertainment to be
held at a location outside California. The cost of the pro-
gram will exceed $10 per person. The association employs at
least one registered lobbyist.

                            QUESTIONS

     1.    Does any provision of the Political Reform Act
of 1974 prohibit a member of the legislature from accepting
the association's invitation and attending its program?

     2.    If the answer to the first question is no, does
the act require the legislator to disclose his acceptance of
the invitation and attendance at the program? If so, what
specific information must he disclose?

     I am requesting this opinion in my capacity as
attorney for a member of the legislature.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [illegible] H. Murphy
                                        Legislative Counsel
